issues.title: Incorrect table reference
issues.problem: The query references a table 'orders' which does not exist in the provided schema. It should reference 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.
issues.fix: SELECT t.transaction_id, t.amount FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t

issues.title: Missing join with dim_merchant
issues.problem: The query does not join with 'sigma_analytics.dim_merchant' to get merchant names instead of merchant_id.
issues.fix: JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Potential performance issue with correlated subquery
issues.problem: The use of a correlated subquery can lead to performance issues as it may not utilize indexes efficiently.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN with a subquery to calculate the average amount per customer_id.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which makes it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql: WITH avg_amounts AS (SELECT customer_id, AVG(amount) AS avg_amount FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions WHERE status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT t.transaction_id, t.amount, m.merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t JOIN avg_amounts aa ON t.customer_id = aa.customer_id JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.amount > aa.avg_amount AND t.status = 'COMPLETED';
review.summary: The query had several critical issues including incorrect table references and missing joins. It also had a performance issue with the correlated subquery and lacked readability due to the absence of comments.
